name: Tonkotsu Ramen
description: 18-hour pork bone broth, chashu, ajitama egg, nori, bamboo shoots & green onion.
backstory: A weary salaryman once stumbled in at 3 AM, soaked from the rain. The Master silently placed this bowl before him. He's been a regular ever since.

name: Yakitori Skewers
description: Charcoal-grilled chicken thigh skewers glazed with house tare sauce, finished with shichimi.
backstory: Two old friends meet here every Friday night. They never talk much — just eat these skewers and share a bottle of sake. Some friendships don't need words.

name: Salmon Onigiri
description: Hand-pressed rice balls with wild salmon, umeboshi, and bonito flake fillings, wrapped in crisp nori.
backstory: A young musician carries these in her guitar case for after-show snacking. She says they taste better at 2 AM — and honestly, the Master agrees.

name: Master's Tamagoyaki
description: Silky rolled omelette made with dashi broth and a touch of mirin, served with grated daikon.
backstory: The Master learned this recipe from his grandmother. He says it's the only dish he's never changed in 30 years. Some things are already perfect.

name: Crispy Gyoza
description: Pan-fried pork and cabbage dumplings with a golden lace crust, served with house ponzu.
backstory: A group of nurses from the hospital down the street come in after every night shift. They always order two rounds. The Master has their table ready by 2:30.

name: Katsu Curry
description: Thick-cut heritage pork cutlet over steamed rice, draped in the Master's slow-simmered curry.
backstory: A retired boxer comes in once a month and orders this. He calls it 'training fuel.' The Master knows it's really just homesickness — this was his mother's recipe.

name: Edamame
description: Steamed young soybeans tossed with Maldon sea salt and a whisper of garlic oil.
backstory: The Master puts this on every table without being asked. He says it's 'the silence before the conversation starts.' Nobody has ever been charged for the first bowl.

name: Miso Soup
description: Red and white miso blend with silken tofu, wakame seaweed, and fresh scallions.
backstory: A young woman walks in every night at midnight sharp, orders only this, reads for exactly one hour, and leaves. The Master has never asked her name.

name: Matcha Tiramisu
description: Layers of matcha-soaked ladyfingers and mascarpone cream, dusted with ceremonial-grade matcha.
backstory: The Master's daughter created this recipe during college. She's a doctor now, but this dish is still on the menu. He swells with quiet pride every time someone orders it.

name: Sake Flight
description: Three curated 60ml pours — junmai, ginjo, and daiginjo — served on a hinoki cypress board.
backstory: A famous author once sat at the counter, drank three of these, and wrote the final chapter of his novel on a napkin. The Master still has that napkin, framed in the back.
